title: Tobacco Taxation And Smoking Prevalence Gaps In Low- And Middle-Income Countries
summary: Build a verified workflow for measuring tobacco taxation levels relative to smoking prevalence and cessation rates in LMICs, linking tax-policy data, prevalence surveys, and illicit-trade estimates at national and sub-national scale.
why_it_matters: Tobacco kills 8 million people annually, 80 percent in LMICs. WHO FCTC recommends tobacco taxes at 75 percent of retail price yet most LMICs are below 50 percent. The tobacco industry argues tax increases fuel illicit trade. Sub-national smoking prevalence data exists from STEPS surveys. Linking tax-policy data to prevalence and illicit-trade estimates enables evidence-based tax policy.

success_metrics.target: Every tax-prevalence estimate links tax, survey, and trade data with methodology.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, safety review, replication.
